# `analysis.architecture.shared-db-coupling` — Shared-database cross-service coupling (SHARES_DATA / SHARES_TABLE_WITH)

Auto-generated. Back to [summary](../summary.md).

- **Language:** [multi](../by-language/multi.md)
- **Category:** [platform](../by-category/platform.md)
- **Subcategory:** App Topology & Integration
- **Capability cells:** 2

## Capabilities

| Capability | Status | Verified at | Issue | Cites | Notes |
|------------|--------|-------------|-------|-------|-------|
| Cross service table coupling | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-14` | — | `cmd/grafel/index.go`<br>`internal/engine/shared_table_coupling_edges.go`<br>`internal/engine/shared_table_coupling_edges_test.go`<br>`internal/types/kinds.go` | #5204 (refs #3628 area #13): the CROSS-SERVICE companion to SHARES_DATA. Where SHARES_DATA models intra-repo module↔module data coupling, this detects when ≥2 DISTINCT services/repos touch the SAME resolved physical table with ≥1 WRITER — the highest-risk hidden coupling a Django→NestJS service split must break apart. New relationship Kind RelationshipKindSharesTableWith=SHARES_TABLE_WITH registered in internal/types/kinds.go + AllRelationshipKinds() (producer-kind validator green). LIVE project-scope engine pass engine.ApplySharedTableCouplingEdges, registered in cmd/grafel/index.go right after Pass 8.8 SHARES_DATA (same data-coupling axis, rides --skip-pass=shared-db). Over the assembled graph it groups by NORMALISED physical table (reusing normTable for schema/alias collapse, e.g. public.orders to orders) from two signals: SCOPE.DataAccess entities (repo + table + operation) and ACCESSES_TABLE edges (caller's repo attributes the access; table/op from edge prop or target DataAccess node). The accessor key is the REPO/service, so same-service multi-module access collapses to one entry and can NEVER fabricate cross-service coupling. operation in {INSERT,UPDATE,DELETE,UPSERT,TRUNCATE,MERGE,CREATE,DROP,ALTER} = write; else read. For each table touched by ≥2 distinct services where ≥1 WRITES, it mints the canonical service:<repo> SCOPE.Service node on demand and emits one SHARES_TABLE_WITH edge per unordered service pair (smaller service ID = FromID; props table, access_from/access_to = read/write kinds per side, writer = from|to|both, confidence=high, provenance=SHARED_TABLE_COUPLING). HONEST: only resolved literal tables (UNKNOWN/dynamic/empty dropped via normTable); read-only pairs are real sharing but not mutable coupling so NO edge; single-service and same-service-two-modules give no edge; append-only, deterministic, idempotent (re-run mints no duplicate). Value-asserting tests: two services both writing orders (one via public.orders) to exactly one edge billing-svc to order-svc writer=both/confidence=high + both service nodes minted; read+write mix to writer points at the writer; same-service-two-modules / single-service / read-only pair / UNKNOWN+empty table to 0 edges; ACCESSES_TABLE-edge attribution path to edge; idempotent re-run to still 1 edge. |
| Shared data coupling | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-02` | — | `cmd/grafel/index.go`<br>`internal/engine/shared_db_coupling.go`<br>`internal/engine/shared_db_coupling_test.go` | #3628 area #13: detects when ≥2 DISTINCT modules access the SAME logical table/collection — the cross-service data-ownership / boundary-violation signal. LIVE project-scope engine pass engine.ApplySharedDataCoupling, registered in cmd/grafel/index.go as Pass 8.8 (PassSharedDB, --skip-pass=shared-db) AFTER module-agg (Pass 8) so synthetic Module nodes exist and every entity carries Properties[module]. Over the assembled graph it converges table/collection accesses by NAME (per repo) from three signals: ACCESSES_TABLE edges (function→SCOPE.DataAccess, table from the DataAccess `table` prop, accessor module from the source/function entity), JOINS_COLLECTION edges (Mongo $lookup, `from` collection), and SCOPE.DataAccess entities directly (module+table props). For each (repo,table) touched by ≥2 distinct REAL-module accessors it stamps shared=true/accessor_count=N/accessor_modules=<sorted csv> on the SCOPE.DataAccess entities and emits a SHARES_DATA edge between every co-accessing Module pair (smaller Module ID = FromID; props coupling=shared_data, shared_tables csv, shared_count, provenance=SHARED_DB_COUPLING). Distinct axis from structural coupling (Pass 8.6 import fan-in/out) and commit-couple (Pass 8.5 temporal). Honest: the `_external`/empty module is never a distinct accessor (unattributed access cannot fabricate coupling); a table touched by ONE module is stamped shared=false with no edge; self-pairs suppressed; skips entirely with no Module graph or no data access. Value-asserting tests: OrderSvc+BillingSvc both ACCESSES_TABLE orders → orders.shared=true/accessor_count=2/accessor_modules=BillingSvc,OrderSvc + exactly one SHARES_DATA edge (OrderSvc,BillingSvc, shared_tables=orders); a single-module table → shared=false/0 edges; JOINS_COLLECTION co-join → coupling edge; idempotent re-run adds no duplicate edge. |
